title: Usopp Runs from the Fight
description: On pages 6–9, usopp runs from the fight focuses on Usopp, Chew, Nami, Arlong as usopp is forced into a one-on-one fight against Chew and struggles between fear, lies, and real courage.
scene: On pages 6–9, usopp runs from the fight focuses on Usopp, Chew, Nami, Arlong as usopp is forced into a one-on-one fight against Chew and struggles between fear, lies, and real courage.

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Well, time to head back...

dialogue.speaker: Kuroobi
dialogue.text: HACHI AND KUROOBI SHOULD'VE TAKEN CARE OF THOSE TRASH BY NOW.

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: turn

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Whew, it worked...! Thank god I made my special "KETCHUP $THR" for cases like this!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: He thinks his water gun hit me just now! Heh heh... now hurry up and go back!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: But what a terrifying race fishermen are...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: After all, they did flip an entire town...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I do feel sorry for Nami,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: And I really do want to help her out but I'd rather not die if possible...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: B-B-But... I already have this ketchup blood on myself.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: I wish I had something more... more umm... more signs that I was in a fierce battle to the death...!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: That's right! I should cover myself with some dirt.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: That'll make me seem more like I was in a fierce fight!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: But what should I say when I get back...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: "My bad but I ended up losing"...?

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Aren't we crewmates?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Or maybe, "Damn it, that guy just barely got away from me!"

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: "Don't cry Nami, it was only natural for us to try and fight."

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: I'd rather die.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: "We've all fought well."

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: It's still a 100 times better than being in a crew that would harm a lady...!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: For the sake of my business, this was the only way.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: "It was a real close fight to the death."

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: "No, no. My wounds are nothing to worry about..."

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: We've been waiting for you, brothers!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Do you understand how hard the choice she made was?

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Let's fight!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Well, if I were to seriously fight...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: I'm so pathetic!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Stop right there you freakin' fish!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: What, so you were still alive...?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Chu (1)

summary: This part pushes the Arlong Park battle forward, clarifying who is fighting, what is at stake, and why Nami’s freedom matters.
